edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:097a5f93b6b1
edges.original_sentence: About 15 years after clear-cutting and 9 years after thinning, slower decomposition of red alder leaves ( Alnus rubra) was reported in streams affected by forest harvesting, which was attributed to lower density of shredder invertebrates.

source_spans.text: About 15 years after clear-cutting and 9 years after thinning, slower decomposition of red alder leaves ( Alnus rubra) was reported in streams affected by forest harvesting, which was attributed to lower density of shredder invertebrates.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:c62da7d67ede
edges.original_sentence: After a further 7 years of forest regrowth (time elapsed between the previous and present study), we found that litter decomposition rate and shredder density in logging-affected reaches converged with values in reference reaches.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:f33084dc6981
edges.original_sentence: Canopy openness was generally higher in reaches without riparian reserves than with 10-m and 30-m reserves and thinned reaches, and it was the lowest in reference reaches.

source_spans.text: Canopy openness was generally higher in reaches without riparian reserves than with 10-m and 30-m reserves and thinned reaches, and it was the lowest in reference reaches.

source_document.title: Assessing the long-term ecological effects of riparian management practices on headwater streams in a coastal temperate rainforest

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:c2dc8f5b61be
edges.original_sentence: Our study indicates that thinning of approximately 20% of the watershed area, including the riparian area, appeared to promote the rate of ecological recovery of streams, when compared to clear-cutting with and without fixed-width reserves in a coastal temperate rainforest in the Pacific Northwest.

source_spans.text: Our study indicates that thinning of approximately 20% of the watershed area, including the riparian area, appeared to promote the rate of ecological recovery of streams, when compared to clear-cutting with and without fixed-width reserves in a coastal temperate rainforest in the Pacific Northwest.

source_document.title: Assessing the long-term ecological effects of riparian management practices on headwater streams in a coastal temperate rainforest

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:f33084dc6981
edges.original_sentence: Among logged sites, 30-m reserves supported the lowest richness compared to sites with 10-m and no reserves ( p = 0.01).

source_spans.text: Among logged sites, 30-m reserves supported the lowest richness compared to sites with 10-m and no reserves ( p = 0.01).

source_document.title: Assessing the long-term ecological effects of riparian management practices on headwater streams in a coastal temperate rainforest
